channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House Democrats in close races try to show they hear voter concerns about immigration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In some of the closest House races in the country, Democratic candidates are leaning into an issue that Republicans have made a centerpiece of their efforts to expand their majority — immigration. Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early in-person voting begins in Arizona, drawing visits from the presidential campaigns
item.description: Early in-person voting is beginning in Arizona, making it the first of this year’s presidential battleground states where all residents can cast a ballot at a traditional polling place ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Sandoval, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Woodward book reveals Trump's calls with Putin and Biden's private remarks on Obama and Netanyahu
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump has had as many as seven private phone calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office and secretly sent the Russian president COVID-19 test machines during the height of the pandemic, Bob Woodward reported in his new book, “War.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Harris continues media blitz with 3 more national interviews
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is continuing her media tour Tuesday with three more interviews for national audiences, with Election Day now just four weeks away.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military board substantiates misconduct but declines to fire Marine who adopted Afghan orphan
item.description: A U.S. Marine whose adoption of an Afghan war orphan has spurred a yearslong legal battle and raised alarms at the highest levels of government will stay in the Marine Corps.
item.dc:creator: Juliet Linderman, Claire Galofaro And Martha Mendoza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US officials reveal fate of nabbed Tajikistan illegal immigrants with alleged ISIS ties
item.description: Eight Tajikistan nationals with alleged ties to ISIS are in the process of being deported from the U.S., officials confirmed to Fox News Digital on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S. That's according to a Justice Department announcement Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: With no government shutdown, it feels like Christmas in September
item.description: This spending bill funds the government through October, November and just before Christmas, setting up another showdown just before Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruz opponent Allred raises $30 million in 3 months
item.description: Rep. Colin Allred (D-Texas) announced Tuesday that he has raised more than $30 million over the past three months in his battle against incumbent Texas Sen. Ted Cruz (R), reflecting broad desire among Democratic donors to defeat one of the Senate’s most prominent conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Overseas meth sent to US election building shuts down office for hours
item.description: The Dutchess County Board of Elections office shut down for hours Monday over a suspicious package from Europe containing crystal meth, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S. Treasury says tribally owned entities should be tax-exempt
item.description: Proposed new guidelines would clarify the decades-old question of whether tribal businesses or entities are exempt from federal taxes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: When was the last time New York voted red?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - New York has been a blue state since the U.S. began labeling states red or blue back in 2000 (more on that later).
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, PIX 11

item.title: Harris and Trump mark somber anniversary of Hamas’ Oct. 7 attack on Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Donald Trump and Vice President Kamala Harris marked the anniversary of the deadliest attack on Jews since the Holocaust as the presidential candidates approach the final weeks of the campaign during a widening conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, COLLEEN LONG and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris is pressed for more specifics in a wide-ranging interview with '60 Minutes'
item.description: The interview followed criticism from former President Donald Trump that Kamala Harris has been unwilling to sit for hard interviews. She was pressed on everything from her economic proposals to immigration.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Exclusive poll shows tight races for NY-17 and NY-18 congressional seats
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New exclusive PIX11 polling with Emerson College and The Hill reveals two incredibly close races and even a ballot controversy just four weeks from election day.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: A visual look at Trump's return to Pennsylvania for a rally at site of assassination attempt
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Thousands of supporters returned to Butler, Pennsylvania, on Saturday to rally around Donald Trump at the same site where a gunman tried to assassinate him in July. Many had lined up as the sun rose for the event billed as a “tribute to the American spirit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is returning to Butler, the site of the 1st assassination attempt against him
item.description: The rally will mark Trump's first time back in the Pennsylvania city since the failed assassination attempt against him in July. The guest list will include a shooting survivor, as well as Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Craig, Teirab tangle over Social Security, abortion in first head-to-head 2nd District debate
item.description: In a policy driven debate, candidates in Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District part on fiscal and abortion policy while finding common ground on response to Jan. 6 Capitol uprising.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth and Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, Fox 5

item.title: Gloria, Turner compete in San Diego mayoral race debate
item.description: The debate between San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria and police officer and challenger Larry Turner covered a wide range of issues from transportation, flood infrastructure failures, housing shortages and of course homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis mayor, City Council clash on homeless shelter funding
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council is launching an inquiry into the city administration’s financial reporting practices following a dispute over funding for a homeless shelter.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: Early voting begins in downtown Chicago
item.description: Early voting for the November election begins Thursday in downtown Chicago, with DuPage County seeing nearly double the amount of voters who turned out early in 2020, and early voting in all 50 wards opens up next Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Meet the candidates running for San Diego City Attorney
item.description: There are only two candidates in the running to become San Diego City Attorney after incumbent Mara Elliott ends her last term in office in December.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5
